ddls:
CREATE TABLE "threads" (
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each thread */,
  "start_time_sec" INTEGER /* Start time of the thread (seconds) */,
  "start_time_nsec" INTEGER /* Start time of the thread (nanoseconds) */,
  "stop_time_sec" INTEGER /* Stop time of the thread (seconds) */,
  "stop_time_nsec" INTEGER /* Stop time of the thread (nanoseconds) */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application the thread belongs to */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service within the application */,
  "thread_type" TEXT /* Type of the thread (e.g., worker, background, critical) */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the thread */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the thread (e.g., running, stopped, error) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("thread_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_threads_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "global_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each global metric entry */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the thread associated with this metric */,
  "global_start_time_sec" INTEGER /* Global start time (seconds) */,
  "global_start_time_nsec" INTEGER /* Global start time (nanoseconds) */,
  "global_stop_time_sec" INTEGER /* Global stop time (seconds) */,
  "global_stop_time_nsec" INTEGER /* Global stop time (nanoseconds) */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application the metric belongs to */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service within the application */,
  "thread_type" TEXT /* Type of the thread */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the thread */,
  "thread_count" INTEGER /* Number of threads involved in the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_global_metrics_thread_id FOREIGN KEY ("thread_id") REFERENCES threads ("thread_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_global_metrics_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "application_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the owner of the application (user) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the application */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the application was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the application was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, manager) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the thread associated with the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert condition */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was created */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., info, warning, critical) */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes related to the resolution of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_thread_id FOREIGN KEY ("thread_id") REFERENCES threads ("thread_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the thread associated with this log */,
  "log_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "execution_time_ms" REAL /* Execution time of the thread in milliseconds */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application the log belongs to */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service within the application */,
  "thread_type" TEXT /* Type of the thread */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the thread */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage during the log entry */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage during the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_logs_thread_id FOREIGN KEY ("thread_id") REFERENCES threads ("thread_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_logs_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thread_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the thread */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the thread */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the status was recorded */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application the status belongs to */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service within the application */,
  "thread_type" TEXT /* Type of the thread */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the thread */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_thread_status_thread_id FOREIGN KEY ("thread_id") REFERENCES threads ("thread_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_thread_status_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application the metrics belong to */,
  "average_execution_time_ms" REAL /* Average execution time in milliseconds */,
  "max_execution_time_ms" REAL /* Maximum execution time in milliseconds */,
  "min_execution_time_ms" REAL /* Minimum execution time in milliseconds */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metric was last updated */,
  "thread_count" INTEGER /* Number of threads involved in the metric */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* Average CPU usage */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Average memory usage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_applications" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the application */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the application (e.g., developer, manager) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_applications_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_applications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action performed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application the log belongs to */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service within the application */,
  "thread_type" TEXT /* Type of the thread */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the thread */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_thread_id FOREIGN KEY ("thread_id") REFERENCES threads ("thread_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance threshold */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated application */,
  "max_execution_time_ms" REAL /* Maximum execution time in milliseconds before an alert is triggered */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the threshold was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the threshold was last updated */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert when the threshold is exceeded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_thresholds_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user who receives the notification */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated alert */,
  "sent_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was sent */,
  "read_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was read */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been read */,
  "dismissed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was dismissed */,
  "is_dismissed" INTEGER /* Indicates if the notification has been dismissed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log */,
  "thread_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the thread that encountered the error */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Description of the error */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error occurred */,
  "application_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application the error log belongs to */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service within the application */,
  "thread_type" TEXT /* Type of the thread */,
  "stack_trace" TEXT /* Stack trace of the error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_application_id FOREIGN KEY ("application_id") REFERENCES applications ("application_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_thread_id FOREIGN KEY ("thread_id") REFERENCES threads ("thread_id")
)
